topic: A neighbor wants to install a fence
raw_output:
[story 1]

The hammering started at seven in the morning, a relentless, rhythmic assault on my peace. I kept glancing out the window, my jaw tight enough to ache. Every time the sound intensified, a knot tightened in my chest, making it hard to draw a full breath. I paced the length of my living room three times before deciding I had to confront the situation. When I finally walked to the shared property line, my voice came out thin and clipped. "Mr. Henderson, could we perhaps discuss the timing of this work? It’s quite disruptive." He just waved a dismissive hand, his smile too wide, and I felt a flush creep up my neck. I could feel the tension coiling tighter and tighter behind my eyes.

[story 2]

I spent the entire afternoon staring at the blueprints he’d left on my mailbox. Lines and measurements swam before my eyes, blurring into an indecipherable mess. Each proposed post placement seemed to invite a fresh wave of anxiety. My hands, resting on the kitchen counter, were clammy. I kept replaying scenarios in my head: the noise levels, the potential for property disputes, the sheer impossibility of getting this sorted smoothly. When he called over later, his cheerful tone grated on my nerves. "Just a few more days, dear," he chirped. I managed a tight nod, my throat feeling constricted, wishing I could just retreat into silence.

[story 3]

The sheer volume of the construction materials piled near the edge of my yard was overwhelming. I watched the workers move with an aggressive efficiency that mirrored the frantic energy churning inside me. I kept rubbing my temples, a dull throb starting behind my eyes. Every shadow seemed too deep, every passing truck too loud. I tried to approach the foreman, but the words caught in my throat, turning to dry, useless air. "I just need to know when this will wrap up," I managed, my voice sounding brittle. The foreman looked up, his expression neutral, and I felt a sudden, sharp urge to walk away, to just disappear from the whole messy spectacle.
